steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Realism meant accurate portrayal. The passage says realism was "a rejection of the romantic style" and that romantics did something "instead." The blank must describe what romantics did to their subjects — the opposite of depicting them accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'mock': means to ridicule — romantics glorified subjects, not mocked them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'exaggerate': means to overstate and magnify — romantics overstated beauty and heroism while hiding flaws, the direct opposite of realist accuracy. ✓
  • Choice C — 'correct': means to fix errors — does not describe an artistic approach to depicting subjects. ✗
  • Choice D — 'undermine': means to weaken — romantics elevated their subjects rather than weakening them. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
